new_beliefs.claim: Framework canon changes only through its own write flow, including at the foundations: the status-lifecycle contract was superseded via the lifecycle it defines (c029 -> c053), and the type enum was replaced through adjudication by a node typed in the values it introduces (c026 -> c051). There is no maintenance hatch, so the completeness of the audit trail is structural rather than behavioral - the history can be trusted without trusting that everyone behaved, because there is no door to bypass it.

evidence.detail: Observed during the four-type schema landing (2026-06-10) and named in the significance exchange the next day: the heaviest self-application load the change discipline has taken - its own type system - ran entirely through preflight, adjudication, and supersession, with the chains walkable via bs history and the migration recorded as a483. Preflight 2026-06-11: dag-schema tag-family overlap only (17 contract-level / 33 schema); reviewed per c055 - the claim attests a demonstrated property of the very contracts matched and contradicts none; a467/c040 review precedent.

new_beliefs.claim: Provide a default-overlay configuration: an operator-local setting that loads the union of the framework graph plus registered private collections on every query, without per-command flags, so private nodes' reverse-deps surface on public nodes by default. The mechanism exists (cross-namespace deps, the union loader, bs dependents over the loaded union); only the UX is missing - the public graph ships clean while the operator's private overlay projects onto it locally at load time.

evidence.detail: From the public/private graph discussion: the user asked whether a proprietary namespaced collection could outbound-dep public cb: nodes and appear on them; the answer is the existing collection mechanism, modulo this default-union convenience. Preflight 2026-06-11: observability tag-overlap with a497 (the lap-log convention, compatible sibling); no semantic conflict.

new_beliefs.claim: Provide a cross-collection desk view: a single query surface for unmaterialized discrete directives across the framework graph and all registered collections, so the live backlog is one command rather than one command per collection.

evidence.detail: First finding of the observation-loop walkthrough: bs list unlinked tag:lifecycle:discrete shows only the targeted graph, so paradigm:a367 was invisible from the framework desk. Persisted as a directive per a496's proportionality rule - an observation with an obvious prescription, no position ceremony. Preflight 2026-06-11: observability tag-overlap with a497 (the lap-log convention, compatible sibling); no semantic conflict.

new_beliefs.claim: Provide a recency view: a query answering what changed in the graph over a given window (new nodes, supersessions, evidence appends, materializations), so concurrent multi-session activity is observable without reading git log on the JSON.

evidence.detail: Second finding of the observation-loop walkthrough, made acute the same day by three sessions writing the repos concurrently. Persisted as a directive per a496. Preflight 2026-06-11: observability tag-overlap with a497 (the lap-log convention, compatible sibling); no semantic conflict.
